text:
Once upon a time, in a small village nestled between rolling hills, there lived a squirrel named Spike.

Spike loved collecting acorns. Every morning he scampered through the trees and searched tirelessly for treasures. But Spike also had big dreams. He often imagined building an incredible oak treehouse in the forest.

One autumn day, Spike discovered something amazing. Beneath an ancient oak tree was a hollow shell full of shiny golden coins. It seemed like fortune had smiled upon him. With those coins, Spike could build his dream!

He gathered acorns day and night, crafting bricks. With imagination as his hammer and imagination as his sculptor’s tool, Spike built walls. Then he added cozy beds made of leaf piles. Soon enough, Spike completed his masterpiece.

Every night he sat inside his treasure house, admiring his creation. Friends came often – rabbits, birds, even a couple of sleepy moles. Spike taught them about history and stories.

One moonlit night, Spike felt sad.
